meta.note: v2.0 pivot: AI chat = main area, conversational job creation, external API integration. B-3 deprecated (replaced by FLOW-1). A-5 upgraded to P0 and merged into FLOW-1. Added ARCH-1, FLOW-1, FLOW-2, FLOW-3.

features.name: Natural language candidate search
features.description: HR can type a free-form recruiting directive (e.g., 'Find 50 senior Go engineers, US timezone') and the Agent initiates a search-and-score pipeline.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can type a natural-language search query into the chat sidebar and the Agent begins retrieving and scoring candidates against the active job's requirements.
test_steps: Open the app; confirm the Agent chat sidebar is visible on the left.
Type a recruiting directive such as 'Find me 50 senior backend engineers with Go + Kubernetes experience, remote-friendly, US/EU timezone'.
Press Enter / Send.
Verify the Agent acknowledges the request and begins processing.
Verify that candidates begin appearing in the associated job's pipeline within a reasonable timeframe.

features.name: Live progress ticker during Agent execution
features.description: While the Agent is searching and scoring, a segmented progress bar and real-time status text (e.g., 'Scoring 34/50...') are displayed in the chat thread.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can see a live progress indicator with step-by-step status text so I know the Agent is working and can estimate completion.
test_steps: Trigger a candidate search via chat.
Observe the chat thread: a segmented horizontal progress bar should appear.
Verify status text updates in real time (e.g., 'Searching...', '50 resumes retrieved', 'Scoring 34/50...').
Verify the progress bar segments fill sequentially as steps complete.
Verify text is rendered in monospace font (JetBrains Mono).

features.name: Structured Action Card output
features.description: Upon completing a task, the Agent renders a structured Action Card in the chat thread containing a result summary and actionable buttons (e.g., 'View Ranking').
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, when the Agent finishes processing, I see a structured Action Card summarizing results with a button that navigates me directly to the Ranking View.
test_steps: Complete a candidate search via the Agent.
Verify an Action Card appears in the chat (left accent border, tertiary surface background).
Verify the card displays a summary (e.g., '12 candidates scored above 85%').
Click the 'View Ranking' button on the card.
Verify navigation to the correct job's /job/:id/pipeline page.

features.name: Job card grid / list view
features.description: The /dashboard page displays all open recruiting positions in a switchable card-grid or compact-list layout.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I see all open positions on the dashboard in a card grid (default) or compact list view, switchable with one click.
test_steps: Navigate to /dashboard.
Verify jobs are displayed in card grid view by default.
Verify each card uses --surface-secondary background with 1px --border-default border.
Click the list-view toggle.
Verify the layout switches to a compact list/table format.
Click the grid-view toggle to switch back and verify restoration.

features.name: Job card data display
features.description: Each job card shows the job title, status badge (Draft/Active/Paused/Closed), total resume count, high-score candidate count, and candidates in 'Interview Scheduled' state.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can glance at any job card and immediately see its status, total candidates, high-score count, and interview-pending count.
test_steps: Navigate to /dashboard with at least one job containing candidates.
Verify the job card displays the job title.
Verify a status badge is visible (one of: Draft, Active, Paused, Closed).
Verify the total resume count is displayed.
Verify the high-score candidate count (score > 80%) is displayed.
Verify the count of candidates in 'Interview Scheduled' state is displayed.

features.name: Left rail JD context tags
features.description: The pipeline page displays a left rail showing the Job Description decomposed into context tags: required skills, nice-to-haves, seniority level.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I see a left rail on the ranking page displaying JD context tags so I never lose sight of what I'm hiring for while reviewing candidates.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline for a job with a defined JD.
Verify a left rail panel is visible.
Verify the rail displays required skill tags.
Verify nice-to-have skills are visually differentiated from required skills.
Verify seniority level is displayed.
Verify the rail remains visible while scrolling the main candidate list.

features.name: Ranked candidate list with scores
features.description: The main panel displays candidates sorted by AI match score descending. Each row shows candidate name/alias, prominent numeric score with horizontal bar, and top 3 matched skill tags.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I see candidates ranked by AI score with prominent score display and skill tags so I can quickly identify top matches.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline with scored candidates.
Verify candidates are listed in descending score order.
Verify each row shows the candidate name or alias.
Verify each row displays the AI match score as a large numeric value.
Verify a horizontal score bar is rendered with correct color (chartreuse for 80-100, amber for 50-79, red for 0-49).
Verify up to 3 matched skill tags are displayed per candidate.
Verify row height is 40-44px and uses alternating --surface-tertiary tint.

features.name: Resume inline preview
features.description: The candidate detail page renders the original resume inline, either as an embedded PDF viewer or a structured timeline extracted from the resume.
features.user_story: As a Hiring Manager, I can view the candidate's original resume rendered inline on their profile page without downloading a separate file.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/candidate/:cid for a candidate with an uploaded resume.
Click or navigate to the Resume tab.
Verify the resume is rendered inline (PDF embed or structured timeline).
If PDF: verify the viewer supports scrolling and zoom.
If timeline: verify work experience entries are displayed chronologically.

features.name: AI Evaluation Report display
features.description: The profile displays the full AI evaluation: overall score, per-dimension breakdown, plain-language reasoning, and explicitly called-out skill gaps.
features.user_story: As a Hiring Manager, I see a transparent AI evaluation with score breakdown, reasoning, and identified skill gaps so I understand why the candidate was ranked at this level.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/candidate/:cid for a scored candidate.
Navigate to the AI Evaluation tab.
Verify the overall match score is prominently displayed.
Verify per-dimension scores are shown (e.g., Technical Fit, Culture Fit, Experience Depth).
Verify plain-language reasoning text is displayed explaining the score.
Verify skill gaps are explicitly listed.

features.name: Pipeline stage transition buttons
features.description: Action buttons on the candidate profile allow the Talent Lead to move the candidate through pipeline stages: Schedule Interview, Reject, Extend Offer, Hire, Archive.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can transition a candidate through hiring stages by clicking action buttons on their profile, and the pipeline updates immediately.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/candidate/:cid.
Verify an action bar with stage-transition buttons is visible.
Click 'Schedule Interview' — verify the candidate's status updates and the button state changes.
Verify the Job Dashboard card counts update to reflect the status change.
Verify the Ranking View reflects the new status.
Test 'Reject' action — verify status updates and destructive styling (--signal-danger).

features.name: Conversation-center layout with collapsible data sidebar [NEEDS REWORK]
features.description: The AI Agent chat occupies the main area (60-70% width, center/left). Data panels (Jobs, Pipeline, Profile) live in a collapsible right-hand sidebar (30-40% width) with tab switching. This is a REWORK of the previous left-sidebar chat layout.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, the AI chat is the primary workspace occupying the center of the screen. Structured data panels (Jobs, Pipeline, Candidate Profile) are accessible via a collapsible right sidebar with tab navigation.
test_steps: Load the app.
Verify the AI chat occupies the main area (60-70% of viewport width).
Verify a right sidebar is visible (30-40% width) with data panels.
Verify the right sidebar has tab switching (Jobs / Pipeline / Profile).
Click the collapse toggle on the right sidebar — verify it collapses and chat goes full-width.
Click again — verify the sidebar expands back.
Resize to tablet width (768-1279px) — verify right sidebar auto-collapses to icon strip.
Resize below 768px — verify chat is full-screen default, with bottom tabs for data panels.
features.rework_note: Previous implementation had chat as left sidebar. Must be rebuilt with chat as main area and data panels as right sidebar. See ARCH-1 for the layout flip feature.

features.name: Dark theme app shell with design tokens
features.description: The entire application uses the defined dark color system (--surface-primary #0D0D0D background) with the specified typography and component styles. No white/light theme.
features.user_story: As a user, the application renders with the defined dark industrial design language — near-black backgrounds, warm off-white text, acid chartreuse accents — from first load.
test_steps: Load the app.
Verify the body background is --surface-primary (#0D0D0D).
Verify primary text color is --text-primary (#E8E8E8).
Verify headings use Space Grotesk font.
Verify body text uses Inter font.
Verify at least one primary CTA button uses --accent-primary (#D4FF00) background.
Verify card components use --surface-secondary (#1A1A1A) background with --border-default (#333333) border.

features.name: Conversational follow-up and refinement
features.description: The Agent supports follow-up messages that refine previous results (e.g., 'Show only candidates with startup experience') without restarting the entire search pipeline.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can ask follow-up questions in natural language and the Agent refines the current result set without starting a new search from scratch.
test_steps: Complete an initial candidate search.
Type a refinement message (e.g., 'Show only candidates with startup experience').
Verify the Agent processes the follow-up in the context of the previous search.
Verify the ranking view updates to reflect the refined results.
Verify the previous Action Card in chat is still visible for reference.

features.name: Agent-drafted JD creation (merged into FLOW-1)
features.description: MERGED INTO FLOW-1. The Agent drafts a complete Job Description from natural-language input, presents it as an editable Action Card, and upon confirmation creates the Job automatically. This is the sole job creation path — no form, no modal.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I describe a hiring need in natural language and the Agent extracts structured fields, generates a full JD preview, and — upon my confirmation — creates the Job and inserts it into the pipeline automatically. No form-filling required.
test_steps: Type a natural-language hiring request (e.g., 'Hire a 3-year energy storage sales rep in Mexico') in the main chat area.
Verify the Agent acknowledges and extracts structured fields (title, location, experience, industry, skills).
Verify a JD Preview Action Card appears in chat with editable fields.
Modify a field inline and verify the change persists.
Click 'Confirm & Create Job'.
Verify the new job appears in the right sidebar Jobs panel.
Verify no form or modal was used in the entire flow.

features.name: Quick-create job modal [DEPRECATED]
features.description: DEPRECATED — Replaced by conversational job creation (FLOW-1). The quick-create modal is removed. Job creation is now exclusively through the AI Agent chat. The '+ New Job' button in the sidebar Jobs panel should redirect focus to the chat input with a pre-filled prompt suggestion.
features.user_story: DEPRECATED. See FLOW-1 for the replacement flow.
test_steps: Navigate to the Jobs panel in the right sidebar.
Verify the '+ New Job' button does NOT open a modal.
Verify clicking '+ New Job' focuses the chat input with a suggested prompt (e.g., 'Describe the role you want to hire for...').
Verify no modal component is rendered anywhere in the job creation flow.
features.deprecated_reason: Replaced by FLOW-1 (conversational job creation). Form-based job creation contradicts the conversation-first paradigm.

features.name: Archive/close job
features.description: A job can be archived or closed, freezing its pipeline and preserving all data. Closed jobs are visually demoted on the dashboard.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can archive or close a job, which freezes its pipeline while preserving all historical data.
test_steps: Navigate to /dashboard.
Open the context menu or settings for an active job.
Select 'Close' or 'Archive'.
Verify the job status badge updates to 'Closed'.
Navigate to the job's pipeline — verify it is in a read-only / frozen state.
Verify the job card is visually demoted (e.g., lower opacity, moved to a separate section).

features.name: Bulk actions on candidates
features.description: Multi-select candidates in the ranking list to perform bulk operations: Advance to Interview, Reject, or Export to CSV.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can multi-select candidates and perform bulk actions so I can efficiently process large candidate pools.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline with multiple candidates.
Select multiple candidates using checkboxes or shift-click.
Verify a bulk action toolbar appears.
Click 'Advance to Interview' — verify all selected candidates' statuses update.
Test 'Reject' bulk action — verify status updates for all selected.
Test 'Export to CSV' — verify a CSV file is downloaded with the selected candidates' data.

features.name: Inline candidate preview expansion
features.description: Clicking a candidate row in the ranking list expands an inline preview panel showing key details, or the user can navigate to the full profile page.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can click a candidate row to see an inline preview without leaving the ranking list, or navigate to the full profile for deep review.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline.
Click on a candidate row.
Verify an inline preview panel expands below/beside the row.
Verify the preview shows key candidate details (score breakdown, top skills, summary).
Verify a 'View Full Profile' link/button is present.
Click 'View Full Profile' — verify navigation to /job/:id/candidate/:cid.

features.name: Sub-score sorting and skill filtering
features.description: The ranking list can be re-sorted by sub-score dimensions (Technical Fit, Culture Fit, Experience Depth) and filtered by specific skill tags.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can re-sort candidates by sub-scores or filter by skill tags to find the best match for specific dimensions.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline with scored candidates.
Click the sort dropdown and select 'Technical Fit'.
Verify the list re-sorts by Technical Fit score descending.
Select a skill tag filter (e.g., 'Kubernetes').
Verify only candidates with that skill tag are shown.
Clear the filter — verify the full list is restored.

features.name: AI-generated interview questions
features.description: The candidate profile includes an Interview Prep tab with AI-generated questions tailored to the candidate's profile mapped against the JD requirements.
features.user_story: As a Hiring Manager, I see AI-generated tailored interview questions so I can prepare targeted interviews based on each candidate's specific profile.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/candidate/:cid.
Click the Interview Prep tab.
Verify a list of AI-generated interview questions is displayed.
Verify questions reference specific aspects of the candidate's experience.
Verify questions map to JD requirements (skills, experience levels).
Verify questions cover identified skill gaps from the evaluation.

features.name: Internal notes on candidate
features.description: Team members can leave internal notes on a candidate profile, visible to all members of the hiring team for that job.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I can leave internal notes on a candidate that are visible to the hiring team, so we can collaborate on evaluation.
test_steps: Navigate to /job/:id/candidate/:cid.
Navigate to the Notes & Activity tab.
Type a note in the input field and submit.
Verify the note appears in the activity feed with author name and timestamp.
Verify the note persists on page reload.
Verify the note is visible when another team member views the same candidate.

features.name: Dashboard filtering by owner/department
features.description: The Job Dashboard supports filtering by job owner and department so users can quickly find relevant positions.
features.user_story: As a Hiring Manager, I can filter the dashboard to see only jobs I own or jobs in my department.
test_steps: Navigate to /dashboard with multiple jobs across departments.
Click the department filter dropdown and select a department.
Verify only jobs in that department are displayed.
Select the 'My Jobs' / owner filter.
Verify only jobs owned by the current user are displayed.
Clear all filters — verify the full job list is restored.

features.name: Chat message styling and distinction
features.description: User messages use --accent-primary background with dark text. Agent messages use --surface-secondary background. No rounded speech-bubble tails — rectangular only.
features.user_story: As a user, I can clearly distinguish my messages from the Agent's messages through distinct visual styling.
test_steps: Open the chat sidebar.
Send a message.
Verify user message has --accent-primary (#D4FF00) background with #0D0D0D text.
Verify the Agent response has --surface-secondary (#1A1A1A) background.
Verify both message types are rectangular (no speech-bubble tails).
Verify no border-radius exceeds 4px.

features.name: Quick-command suggestions in chat
features.description: The chat sidebar displays contextual quick-command suggestion chips (e.g., 'Find candidates', 'Create job', 'Summarize pipeline') that the user can tap to pre-fill the input.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I see suggested quick-commands in the chat sidebar so I can discover available actions without memorizing commands.
test_steps: Open the chat sidebar on an empty state or after completing a task.
Verify suggestion chips are displayed (e.g., 'Find candidates', 'Create job').
Click a suggestion chip.
Verify the chip's text pre-fills the chat input field.
Verify suggestions are contextual (different suggestions on /dashboard vs /job/:id/pipeline).

features.name: Dashboard render under 1.5 seconds
features.description: The /dashboard and ranking list pages must fully render (first contentful paint + data populated) within 1.5 seconds on a 4G connection.
features.user_story: As a user on a moderate connection, the dashboard and ranking pages load quickly enough that I never feel like I'm waiting.
test_steps: Open Chrome DevTools, set network throttling to 'Fast 3G' or '4G' simulation.
Navigate to /dashboard.
Measure time from navigation start to Largest Contentful Paint (LCP).
Verify LCP is under 1500ms.
Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline with 100+ candidates.
Verify LCP is under 1500ms.

features.name: Chat streaming response under 500ms
features.description: When the user sends a message, the first token of the Agent's streaming response must appear within 500ms.
features.user_story: As a user, when I send a message the Agent starts responding almost immediately so the interaction feels conversational.
test_steps: Open the chat sidebar.
Type and send a message.
Measure time from send to first visible token of the Agent's response.
Verify first token appears within 500ms.
Repeat 3 times to confirm consistency.

features.name: Virtualized list for 10k+ candidates
features.description: The candidate ranking list uses virtualized rendering to support 10,000+ candidates per job pipeline without UI degradation or excessive memory usage.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead working on a high-volume role, the ranking list remains smooth and responsive even with 10,000+ candidates.
test_steps: Load a job pipeline with 10,000+ candidates (use test data seeding).
Scroll through the ranking list.
Verify scrolling remains smooth (no dropped frames visible).
Check browser memory usage — verify it does not exceed 500MB.
Verify only a subset of DOM rows are rendered at any time (inspect DOM element count).

features.name: Keyboard navigation for all interactive elements
features.description: All interactive elements (buttons, links, form controls, table rows, modals) are focusable and operable via keyboard alone. Focus indicators are visible.
features.user_story: As a user who relies on keyboard navigation, I can access and operate every feature without a mouse.
test_steps: Start at /dashboard. Press Tab repeatedly.
Verify focus moves through all interactive elements in logical order.
Verify focus indicator is clearly visible (not hidden or barely visible).
Navigate into the chat sidebar via keyboard and send a message using Enter.
Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline and use arrow keys / Tab to move through the candidate list.
Open a modal (e.g., '+ New Job') using keyboard and verify it traps focus correctly.

features.name: Screen reader support for data tables
features.description: All data tables (ranking list, dashboard grid) have proper ARIA labels, row/column headers, and live regions for dynamic updates.
features.user_story: As a screen reader user, I can understand and navigate the candidate ranking table and job dashboard with full context announced.
test_steps: Enable a screen reader (VoiceOver / NVDA).
Navigate to /dashboard — verify job cards are announced with title, status, and counts.
Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline — verify the table has proper headers announced.
Verify candidate rows announce name, score, and skill tags.
Trigger a candidate search via chat — verify the live region announces when results are ready.

features.name: Resume data encryption at rest
features.description: All uploaded resume files and extracted candidate data are encrypted at rest in the database and file storage layer.
features.user_story: As an organization handling sensitive candidate data, resume files and personal information are encrypted at rest to meet compliance requirements.
test_steps: Upload a resume via the application.
Verify the file is stored in an encrypted storage bucket (Supabase storage encryption or equivalent).
Query the database directly — verify candidate PII fields are not stored in plaintext.
Verify the application's Supabase project has Row Level Security (RLS) enabled.
Verify SSL/TLS is enforced for all database connections.

features.name: GDPR candidate deletion
features.description: Candidates can be fully anonymized or deleted on request, removing all PII from the database, file storage, and AI evaluation records.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead responding to a GDPR deletion request, I can fully anonymize or delete a candidate's data from all systems.
test_steps: Navigate to a candidate's profile.
Trigger the 'Delete / Anonymize' action (via settings or admin panel).
Verify the candidate's name, contact info, and resume file are removed.
Verify the candidate row in the ranking list shows 'Anonymized' or is removed.
Verify the AI evaluation report for this candidate no longer references PII.
Verify the deletion is logged in an audit trail.

features.name: Externalized strings for i18n readiness
features.description: All user-facing strings are externalized into locale files (English default). The architecture supports future addition of CJK and RTL language packs without code changes to components.
features.user_story: As a product team planning international expansion, all UI strings are externalized so adding new languages requires only translation files, not code changes.
test_steps: Verify a locale directory or i18n configuration exists in the project.
Verify all user-facing strings in components reference externalized keys, not hardcoded text.
Add a test locale file (e.g., pseudo-locale with prefixed strings).
Switch the app to the test locale and verify all visible strings update.
Verify date and number formats respect locale settings.
Verify the layout does not break when strings are 50% longer (simulating German/French expansion).

features.name: Responsive mobile layout with bottom tab navigation
features.description: On screens below 768px (including Capacitor iOS/Android), the app switches to a bottom tab navigation with Chat, Dashboard, and Pipeline as primary tabs. Sidebar is hidden; chat becomes a full-screen tab.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead using the mobile app, I can navigate between Chat, Dashboard, and Pipeline via a bottom tab bar that feels native on iOS/Android.
test_steps: Open the app on a mobile viewport (below 768px) or in Capacitor simulator.
Verify a bottom tab bar is visible with Chat, Dashboard, and Pipeline icons.
Verify the desktop sidebar is NOT visible.
Tap each tab — verify the correct view loads.
Verify the active tab has --accent-primary highlight.
Verify safe area insets are respected on iOS (notch, home indicator).

features.name: Swipe gestures on candidate cards
features.description: On mobile, candidate rows in the ranking list support swipe gestures: swipe right to advance to interview, swipe left to reject. Haptic feedback confirms the action.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead on mobile, I can quickly process candidates by swiping cards left (reject) or right (advance), with haptic feedback confirming my action.
test_steps: Open /job/:id/pipeline on a mobile viewport or Capacitor simulator.
Swipe a candidate card to the right.
Verify a green 'Advance' action is revealed and triggers on full swipe.
Verify haptic feedback fires on action trigger (Capacitor only).
Swipe a candidate card to the left.
Verify a red 'Reject' action is revealed and triggers on full swipe.
Verify the candidate's status updates after swipe action.

features.name: Pull-to-refresh on all list views
features.description: On mobile, all list views (Dashboard, Ranking, Chat) support pull-to-refresh gesture to reload data.
features.user_story: As a mobile user, I can pull down on any list to refresh the data without hunting for a reload button.
test_steps: Open /dashboard on mobile viewport.
Pull down on the job list.
Verify a refresh indicator appears and data reloads.
Navigate to /job/:id/pipeline and pull-to-refresh.
Verify the candidate list refreshes with latest data.
Verify the refresh indicator animation is smooth.

features.name: Layout flip — AI chat as main area, data panels as right sidebar
features.description: Invert the current layout: AI Agent chat becomes the primary interaction surface occupying 60-70% of viewport width (center/left). Dashboard, Pipeline, and Candidate Profile become panels inside a collapsible right-hand sidebar (30-40% width) with tab switching between them. Mobile: chat is full-screen default, data panels via bottom tabs.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, the AI conversation is my primary workspace. I see the chat in the main area of the screen. I can reference Jobs, Pipeline, and Candidate data in a collapsible right sidebar without leaving the conversation.
test_steps: Load the app on desktop (1280px+).
Verify the AI chat area occupies 60-70% of the viewport width.
Verify the right sidebar occupies 30-40% of the viewport width.
Verify the right sidebar has tab buttons for Jobs / Pipeline / Profile.
Click each tab — verify the corresponding data panel loads in the sidebar.
Click the sidebar collapse toggle — verify the sidebar hides and chat expands to full width.
Click again — verify the sidebar re-expands.
Resize to tablet (768-1279px) — verify the sidebar collapses to an icon strip.
Resize to mobile (<768px) — verify chat is full-screen with bottom tab navigation for data panels.
Verify the previous left-sidebar chat layout is completely removed.

features.name: Conversational job creation — natural language to structured JD to auto-create
features.description: HR describes a hiring need in natural language (e.g., 'Hire a 3-year energy storage sales rep in Mexico'). The Agent extracts structured fields (title, location, experience level, industry, required skills), generates a full JD preview as an editable Action Card in the chat, and upon user confirmation, auto-creates the Job and inserts it into the Pipeline. Replaces the deprecated B-3 quick-create modal. This is the ONLY job creation path.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, I describe what I need to hire in plain language. The Agent understands my intent, structures it into a proper JD, shows me a preview I can edit, and creates the job when I confirm. No forms, no modals.
test_steps: Type a natural-language hiring request in the chat (e.g., 'I need to hire a senior Go engineer in Berlin with 5 years experience').
Verify the Agent acknowledges and shows a progress indicator while processing.
Verify the Agent presents a JD Preview Action Card containing: extracted title, location, experience level, skills, and a generated job description.
Verify the JD Preview fields are editable inline.
Modify the title field and verify the change persists in the preview.
Click 'Confirm & Create Job'.
Verify the job appears in the right sidebar Jobs panel with status 'Draft'.
Verify a confirmation message appears in chat with a link to the new job.
Verify no modal or form was used at any point.
Try creating another job via chat — verify the flow is repeatable.

features.name: External API integration — Coresignal/Apollo.io real candidate search
features.description: Integrate Coresignal (primary) or Apollo.io (alternative) APIs to search for real candidate profiles based on structured JD parameters. The Agent constructs API queries from JD fields (title, skills, experience, location, industry), executes the search, and retrieves real professional profiles. Replaces all mock candidate data in the sourcing pipeline.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, when I ask the Agent to find candidates for a job, it searches real professional databases (Coresignal/Apollo.io) and returns actual candidate profiles — not mock data.
test_steps: Create a job via conversational flow (FLOW-1).
Ask the Agent to find candidates for the job (e.g., 'Find candidates for this role').
Verify the Agent constructs search parameters from the JD (visible in progress updates).
Verify the Agent calls the external API (Coresignal or Apollo.io).
Verify real candidate profiles are returned (not mock/seed data).
Verify candidate data includes: name, work history, education, skills, location.
Verify API errors are handled gracefully (show cached results or informative error message).
Verify API keys are not exposed in the client-side network tab.
Verify rate limiting is respected (no excessive API calls for the same query).

features.name: Agent resume structuring + AI scoring and ranking (replaces mock data)
features.description: The Agent takes raw candidate profiles from external APIs, normalizes them into HireWise's internal Candidate schema, performs multi-dimensional AI scoring (Technical Fit, Culture Fit, Experience Depth, Leadership Potential) against the JD, generates plain-language reasoning traces for each score, and presents ranked results in the Pipeline panel. Fully replaces the current mock scoring system.
features.user_story: As a Talent Lead, after the Agent retrieves real candidates, it automatically structures their data, scores them against the JD with transparent reasoning, and presents a ranked list I can act on immediately.
test_steps: After FLOW-2 retrieves real candidates, verify the Agent structures raw API data into normalized profiles.
Verify each candidate has: structured work history, education, skills list, location.
Verify AI scoring runs automatically after structuring.
Verify each candidate receives an overall match score (0-100).
Verify per-dimension scores are generated: Technical Fit, Culture Fit, Experience Depth, Leadership Potential.
Verify plain-language reasoning is generated for each dimension score.
Verify skill gaps are identified and listed.
Verify candidates are ranked by overall score descending in the Pipeline panel.
Verify the Pipeline panel in the right sidebar updates with real scored candidates.
Verify an Action Card summary appears in chat (e.g., '25 candidates found, 8 scored above 85%').
Verify no mock data is used anywhere in the scoring pipeline.
